chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمَرْأَةِ يَمْنَعُهَا زَوْجُهَا مِنْ حَجَّةِ الْإِسْلَامِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 44 – The woman who is forbidden by her husband from performing the Hajj of Al-Islam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/173)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/173)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ امْرَأَةٍ لَهَا زَوْجٌ وَ هِيَ صَرُورَةٌ لَا يَأْذَنُ لَهَا فِي الْحَجِّ قَالَ تَحُجُّ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَأْذَنْ لَهَا .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa, from Aban, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about a woman having a husband for her, and she is a Sarourat (not having had performed Hajj), who is not permitting to her for the Hajj’. Heasws said: ‘She should perform Hajj, and even if he has not permitted to her’.
en.sarwar: 3. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Washsha’ from Aban from Zurarah who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a woman who has a husband and it is her first al-Hajj but he does not give her permission to go for al-Hajj. He (the Imam) said, ‘She can perform al-Hajj even though he does not give permission.”
